page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 80 vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the instance utilizes dedicated Intel Xeon processors running at 2.2 GHz, featuring 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor cache configuration includes 1280 KB of L1d cache, 10 MB of L2 cache, and 110 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by a tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 10 percent, and single-threaded compression tasks also score poorly. However, multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores reach average levels. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as read-only cached and uncached operations, perform in the middle 50 percent, though the overall Passmark memory score is weak, placing in the bottom 25 percent.

Given its massive memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 24 GB per core, this instance offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring vast memory footprints rather than raw single-threaded compute speed. The server is suited for large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads that can leverage its multi-threaded capacity while tolerating lower single-core performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 80 vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 40 physical cores. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks are poor, ranking in the bottom 10 percent, whereas multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations achieve average tier results. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 24 GB per core, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is a memory-optimized instance with 80 vCPUs, 1922 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, offering high memory density for database and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM capacity and dedicated multi-core processing for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 80 vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM driven by a 2.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Dedicated x86_64 architecture with 40 physical cores and hyper-threading
Average multi-core CPU, database, and Redis SET benchmark performance
Tradeoff of lower single-core performance and weak memory benchmark scores
Zero local storage and no bundled GPU accelerators
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and memory-intensive workloads
